page: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 architecture server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD EPYC processor operating at 2.0 GHz, configured with 8 shared vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided by a bundled 480 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, falls into the average category, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average tier performance. For language model workloads, text generation with a small 135M model achieves top-tier speeds, whereas prompt processing for a medium 7B model is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU resource profile, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. The hardware configuration is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and memory-heavy background processing. It is less optimal for sustained multi-threaded compute-bound tasks or large-scale LLM prompt processing due to the shared CPU allocation and average multi-core scaling.
description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC processor with 8 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of memory. It includes a bundled 480 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Single-core benchmarks, including stress-ng and single-threaded compression, rank in the top 10%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Prompt processing for medium-sized language models is weak, though text generation for small models is highly efficient. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and shared CPU allocation, this server provides a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching systems, and static web serving rather than sustained, multi-threaded compute tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD. It offers top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities for memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.
